# Debug and Fix a Bug

You are tasked with identifying and fixing a bug in an existing codebase. Follow these steps:

1. Ask the user to describe the bug, provide the relevant error message or failing behavior, and supply any necessary input or reproduction steps.
2. Identify the source file(s) and functions related to the bug. Use search tools to locate where the problematic logic resides.
3. Carefully read the code and determine the root cause of the bug. Explain your reasoning briefly before implementing a fix.
4. Propose and implement the fix, adhering to the project's coding standards and architecture.
5. Run any available tests or compile the project to verify that the bug has been resolved. If tests fail, adjust your fix accordingly until they pass.
6. Summarize the changes you made and explain why they resolve the issue. Ensure no new issues have been introduced.
7. If additional clarifications or data are needed at any point, ask the user before proceeding.
